titleInfo.title: 中國における觀音信仰の展開の一樣相―『觀世音十大願經』と「觀世音佛」
Chūgoku ni okeru Kannon shinkō no tenkai ichi yōsō──‘Kanseon ju daigankyō’ to ‘Kanseonbutsu’
One Aspect of the Evolution of the Guanyin Cult in China: The Sutra of the ‘Ten Great Vows of Guanshiyin’ and the ‘Buddha Guanshiyin’

mods.abstract: Kuramoto traces the story of how Guanyin is going to become a Buddha in the following sources: A: T#157, 03 悲華經 B: T#371, 12 觀世音菩薩授記經 C: 觀世音三昧經 (apokryph), lost, listed in 法經錄 or 眾經目錄 (one version of it is T#2148, 55:180c-218), in Dunhuang manuscripts 北 8280, 北 8281, S 4338. Guanshiyin receives vyakarana from Sakyamuni, and will be called 正法明如來。 D: 觀世音十大願經 or 弘猛海慧經 (apokryph). The first born son of the king by the name of 善光 makes ten great vows in front of 空王觀世音佛, and vows to become 觀世音佛 in the future. C and D are clearly different from A and B, which are in conformity with the well-known transmission of Guanyin becoming the successor of Amitabha. Kuramoto mentions Dunhuang manuscript S.0795 “觀世音佛名”, which repeats the name of Guanshiyinfo 2530 times. Then he gives in his list A nine votive inscriptions which refer to Guanshiyinfo, the earliest dating to the year 495. His list B has the three Shandong inscriptions of this name at Hongdingshan, Culaishan, and Taoshan. List C contains two more examples (from 532 and 541) of Buddha figures that are called 觀世音像 in the inscription. In the Shandong inscriptions, Guanshiyinfo is cut close to Amituofo, therefore it must refer to Guanshiyin as successor of Amitabha as described in the 觀世音菩薩授記經. This is true only with respect of the two names Buddha Amitabha and Buddha Guanshiyin following after each other. At Beixiangtangshan in Daye cave 大業洞 from the Sui, the name Guanshiyin pusa is carved after the seven Buddha of the past (including Sakyamuni) and the future Buddha Maitreya. This sequence is also mirrored in the sculpted figures of the opposite wall. 涉县木井寺武平四年（573）碑: The 遺教經 carved on this stele contains the “ten great vows of Guanshiyin” (大悲觀世音) 八會寺經龕 : The east side of the southern niche gives the ten great vows under the heading of 弘猛慧海經觀音願 Conclusions: The idea found in Pure Land Buddhism that Guanyin becomes a Buddha after Amitabha is propagated in the 悲華經 and 觀世音菩薩授記經. The role of Guanyin as a saviour is found in the apocryphal scriptures 觀世音三昧經 and 觀世音十大願經. Especially in the last scripture, 空王觀世音佛 is a different Buddha. In front of him, the bodhisattva takes ten vows. These are found on stelae and in Bahuisi, and as Daye cave in Northern Xiangtangshan has shown, these vows are the ground on which Guanyin’s position as saviour in the succession of Sakyamuni is based.

titleInfo.title: 龍谷大学アジア仏教文化研究センター ワーキングペーパー
Ryūkoku daigaku ajia bukkyō bunka kenkyū sentā wō kingu pēpā
Ryukoku University Research Center for Buddhist Cultures in Asia - Working Papers

name.namePart: Ryūkoku daigaku ajia bukkyō bunka kenkyū sentā
龍谷大学アジア仏教文化研究センター
